title: URIs, URL Resolution, Origins, DNS, and Destination Identity · Browser and Node Client Systems · Compiler Case: Origin Authority Host
summary: A Node-based partner SDK accepts tenant endpoints and must preserve URL, origin, IDN, DNS, and certificate distinctions across environments. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts origin authority host increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch destination-resolution and origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare origins from scheme, host, and effective port while separating URI authority, Host, and connection address. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Encode path segments and query names or values at the correct structural layer without double encoding. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
